author: jtraglia
content:
* Why exactly would just storing the PTC for the last slot of the previous epoch be uglier?
> We could do this by keeping only one PTC, that of the last slot of the previous epoch, in the state, but that's even uglier.

author: jtraglia
content: * I feel that "lookbehind" is a bit misleading in both PRs, since they include the current slot/epoch's PTC too. Would you be open to changing this? Not sure what would be better, maybe `ptc_cache`?

author: jtraglia
content:
Ah I found the answer to this in the first PR:
> yes, this is an option that was proposed, it's also possible to have it of size 1! but they both look pretty ugly to me. It forces clients (and spec) to have special cases per slots. Both testing and implementations become more fragile by this I believe.

author: jtraglia
content: I would also like to define `PTC_CACHE_PREVIOUS_SLOT_INDEX` (0) and `PTC_CACHE_CURRENT_SLOT_INDEX` (1) constants.

author: jtraglia
content: Such a change would look like: https://gist.github.com/jtraglia/8c8386ddfc3bf51c7d5d949f74f1c758

author: potuz
content: if you do this then you may as well remove the Vector[Vector,2]] and just keep the previous PTC and current PTC in the state separately as I said in the comment

author: potuz
content: It's less verbose than having to deal with extra named constant then for specrefs

author: jtraglia
content: Yeah I'd be fine with that too.
